definitions.text: In, on, or at this place (a place perceived to be close to the speaker); compare there.
examples: You wait here while I fetch my coat.
Ms. Doe is not here at the moment.

definitions.text: At this point or stage (in a process, argument, narration, etc.)
examples: I've had enough of peeling potatoes. I'm stopping here.
You say that all men are born equal, but here I must disagree with you.
I'm not dead yet! I'm still here!
Here is where I met my spouse twelve years ago.
An Alzheimer patient's here may in his mind be anywhere he called home in the time he presently re-lives.

definitions.text: This point or stage in time, conceptualised as a location.
examples: I'd like to continue my story, but here is where I must stop.
I've done as much as I can; you'll have to take it from here.

definitions.text: (Can we verify this sense?) This time, the present situation. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
Used semi-assertively to offer something to the listener.
examples: Here, now I'm giving it to you.

definitions.text: Used for emphasis at the beginning of a sentence when expressing an opinion or want.
examples: Here, I'm tired and I want a drink.

definitions.text: Used to attract someone's attention, often in a hostile way.
